User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the BookYourBrands CRM, encompassing admins, team members, and clients.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

role.description: The user's role within the system (admin, team, client).

profilePic.description: URL or reference to the user's profile picture.

telegramChatId.description: The user's unique Telegram chat ID for notifications.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project managed within the BookYourBrands CRM.

id.description: Unique identifier for the project entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client associated with this project. (Relationship: Client 1:N Project)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the project was created.

status.description: The current status of the project (e.g., Active, In Progress, Completed).

coverImage.description: URL or reference to the projects cover image.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task within a project in the BookYourBrands CRM.

id.description: Unique identifier for the task entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this task belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N Task)

assignedTo.description: Reference to the User (Team Member/Editor) assigned to this task. (Relationship: User 1:N Task)

dueDate.description: The due date for the task.

status.description: The current status of the task (Pending, In Progress, Completed).

comments.description: Optional comments associated with the task.

Chat.title: Chat
Chat.description: Represents a chat room associated with a project in the BookYourBrands CRM.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this chat belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:1 Chat)

messages.description: Array of message IDs within this chat.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a single message within a chat room.

id.description: Unique identifier for the message entity.

chatId.description: Reference to the Chat this message belongs to. (Relationship: Chat 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the User who sent the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

text.description: The text content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

fileURL.description: URL of any file attached to the message (optional).

File.title: File
File.description: Represents a file uploaded and managed within the BookYourBrands CRM.

id.description: Unique identifier for the file entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this file is associated with. (Relationship: Project 1:N File)

uploadedBy.description: Reference to the User who uploaded the file. (Relationship: User 1:N File)

fileType.description: The type of file (e.g., video, document, image).

fileURL.description: URL of the stored file in Firebase Storage.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification for a user.

projectId.description: Reference to the project the notification is about. Can be 'general'.

read.description: Whether the user has read the notification.

recipients.description: Array of user IDs who should receive the notification.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Only the authenticated user can access their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores project information. Includes the 'clientId' for associating the project with a specific client.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project.

definition.description: Stores tasks associated with a specific project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier of the task.

definition.description: Stores chat room information for a project.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat.

definition.description: Stores messages within a chat room.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the message.

definition.description: Stores file metadata associated with projects.  Admin and assigned team members can read, Clients can only read their own files.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the file.

definition.description: Stores notifications for users. Rules ensure users can only read their own notifications.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the notification.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure, scalable, and easily debuggable CRM for BookYourBrands. It prioritizes authorization independence by denormalizing authorization data and ensures clear separation of concerns through structural segregation. Access modeling follows consistent patterns, leveraging path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative entities. The structure supports QAPs by using segregation and predictable data patterns, allowing secure list operations and minimizing the need for complex filtering in rules.

**Authorization Independence:** The `clientId` is stored within the `projects` documents. This allows rules to validate ownership without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the `clients` collection.

**Structural Segregation:** The project documents are stored at the top level, making them accessible for all users, while tasks are nested under projects, and chats are separated, keeping different levels of access separate.

**Access Modeling:** The `/users/{userId}` path is used for private user data, ensuring only the user can access their information. Projects are associated with clients through the `clientId` field. Tasks are nested under projects to simplify project-specific access control. Chats and Messages are segregated for clarity. The new `/notifications` collection allows for scalable, user-specific notifications.

**QAPs Support:** By storing projects at the top level, listing projects is possible with rules that ensure the user has access to the projects based on their role. Querying notifications is efficient because they are queried by the recipient's user ID.
